video:video.video:title: Asif Ali and Saagar Shaikh on return of 'Deli Boys' | AP interview
video:video.video:description: Asif Ali and Saagar Shaikh are back for more "Deli Boys" as the comedy begins streaming its second season on Hulu and Disney+.

video:video.video:title: Thalía set free with ’Todo Suena Mejor en Cumbia’ | AP interview
video:video.video:description: Thalía says "everything sounds better in cumbia." The Latin pop star's 17th studio album "Todo Suena Mejor en Cumbia" features original songs and covers.

video:video.video:description: Steven Spielberg's “Disclosure Day" returns Hollywood’s preeminent big-screen craftsman to one of his most abiding questions: Are we alone?

video:video.video:title: Packers’ Micah Parsons offers more conservative update regarding his potential return
video:video.video:description: Green Bay Packers edge rusher Micah Parsons discusses a possible timeline for his return from a torn left anterior cruciate ligament that ended his 2025 season prematurely.

video:video.video:title: IRS may owe you a refund
video:video.video:description: If you were penalized by the IRS during the pandemic for failing to pay your taxes or filing late, you may qualify for a refund or termination of the penalties.

video:video.video:title: Joint investigation into ICE detainees describing medical neglect
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of detainees across at least 33 states allege in federal lawsuits that immigration detention facilities are failing to provide adequate medical care, an investigation by KFF Health News and The Associated Press exposes.

video:video.video:title: Christian Watson discusses personal goals for the 2026 season
video:video.video:description: Green Bay Packers wide receiver Christian Watson discusses personal goals for the 2026 season now that he has signed a four-year contract extension.

video:video.video:description: UFC heavyweight Derrick Lewis on fighting at the White House

video:video.video:title: Green Bay Packers' Tucker Kraft on coming back from injury
video:video.video:description: Green Bay Packers tight end Tucker Kraft believes he should return to peak form once he's back on the field after recovering from a torn anterior cruciate ligament.

video:video.video:title: Lukas Van Ness discusses the trajectory of his career
video:video.video:description: Green Bay Packers edge rusher Lukas Van Ness discusses the trajectory of his career since getting selected in the first round of the 2023 draft.

video:video.video:title: Missile lands in field near Damascus after Israel-Iran exchange of fire
video:video.video:description: A missile lay partially buried in a field on the outskirts of Damascus on Monday, surrounded by scorched earth after overnight exchanges of fire between Israel and Iran sent projectiles across the region.

video:video.video:title: Supreme leader's message urges Iranians to 'stand firm' as they mark Khomeini death anniversary
video:video.video:description: Iran's supreme leader warned that the country’s enemies, after what he described as battlefield setbacks, are pursuing “hybrid warfare” aimed at weakening public resilience and influencing government decision-making.

video:video.video:title: Putin answers series of questions from international media alongside St Petersburg Economic Forum
video:video.video:description: President Vladimir Putin held a meeting with the heads of international news agencies on Thursday and said that Russia will strengthen its air defenses to counter recent Ukrainian drone attacks, which have reached deep inside the country.

video:video.video:title: Foreign workers say they were paid less than $2 an hour to build new US consulate in Milan
video:video.video:description: Italian prosecutors are investigating Alabama-based Caddell Construction after workers allege labor exploitation, according to The Associated Press interviews with five former employees and a review of their employment letters and pay stubs.

video:video.video:title: Retired president Raúl Castro turns 95 as Cuba celebrates enigmatic figure
video:video.video:description: Raúl Castro has turned 95, a landmark birthday for a man still helping lead one of the last communist countries in the world.

video:video.video:title: What stars say you can't have the World Cup without
video:video.video:description: You can't have a World Cup without... tequila? Here's what the stars think.

video:video.video:title: 'Disclosure Day' cast invades London
video:video.video:description: Steven Spielberg and the cast of "Disclosure Day" land in London for the sci-fi epic's UK premiere.

video:video.video:title: After escaping the Taliban and years in exile, Afghanistan women's soccer team rises again
video:video.video:description: The Afghanistan soccer federation doesn’t recognise the women’s team which played its last official competitive match in 2018.

video:video.video:title: Footage captures severe winds collapsing a large tent in West Virginia University ball park
video:video.video:description: Powerful winds tossed a large white tent at a college baseball game in West Virginia in a burst of severe weather that injured five people, according to officials with West Virginia University.

video:video.video:title: Balkan leaders attend EU summit in Montenegro as enlargement gains urgency
video:video.video:description: The Balkans are seen as crucial in countering security and economic threats from Russia and China. High on the agenda was Montenegro's potential EU accession.

video:video.video:title: ICE detainee's daughter describes medical neglect in immigration detention centers
video:video.video:description: An investigation by The AP and KFF Health News found immigrant detainees in 33 states alleged the U.S. is failing to provide them with adequate medical care.

video:video.video:title: NATO chief learns of UK defense secretary's resignation during news conference
video:video.video:description: NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte, informed of Healey's resignation by The Associated Press during a news conference in Brussels, said that Healey is someone “I respect very much.”

video:video.video:title: Authorities say a man barricaded himself inside a bank in California along with several others
video:video.video:description: Police say a man barricaded himself inside a bank building in the Southern California city of Bakersfield with an unknown number of people.

video:video.video:title: FIFA opens international broadcast center ahead of 2026 World Cup
video:video.video:description: FIFA officially opened its International Broadcast Center in Dallas on Monday, unveiling the facility that will serve as the broadcast and technology hub for the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

video:video.video:title: Graham Platner wins the Democratic primary for US Senate in Maine, will face GOP Sen. Susan Collins
video:video.video:description: Graham Platner won the Maine Democratic primary for U.S. Senate on Tuesday, setting up a high-stakes race against Republican Sen. Susan Collins.

video:video.video:title: Rubio testifies before Congress for the first time since the start of the Iran war
video:video.video:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio faced a litany of questions about the Trump administration’s fragile or stalling diplomatic efforts around the world.

video:video.video:title: Brunson thanks his teammates after Knicks take Game 1 of the NBA Finals
video:video.video:description: Jalen Brunson praised his teammates after the Knicks took Game 1 of the NBA Finals — but said the team is staying focused on what comes next.

video:video.video:title: Iran and Israel say they have halted strikes after first exchange of fire since ceasefire
video:video.video:description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu acknowledged a halt in fighting with Iran on Monday, but vowed to respond "with force" to future attacks.

video:video.video:title: New York fans celebrate Knicks' historic Game 4 comeback win
video:video.video:description: The New York Knicks made a record comeback from 29 points down and moved to the brink of their first championship since 1973 by beating the San Antonio Spurs 107-106 in Game 4 of the NBA Finals.

video:video.video:title: Top Republican hopeful for California governor Steve Hilton reacts to primary results
video:video.video:description: Steve Hilton, a former Fox News host and top Republican contender for California governor, doubled down on a message he kept revisiting throughout his campaign as he waited for ballots to be tallied: (AP Video by Terry Chea)

video:video.video:title: Stocks slump as Big Tech sinks and a strong May jobs report boosts odds for higher interest rates
video:video.video:description: The U.S. stock market had its worst day since October as a sell-off in big technology companies weighed down the broader market.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he'll back Sen. Collins in Maine, blasting Platner as a 'thug' and 'pig'
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says he’s backing Republican incumbent Susan Collins in the Maine Senate race despite their differences, bashing her opponent, Democrat Graham Platner, as a "thug" and an "outright pig."

video:video.video:title: Israel tightens its grip on east Jerusalem with evictions and demolitions
video:video.video:description: Fakhri Abu Diab fought for decades to save his home. But when Israeli authorities arrived with bulldozers two years ago, he was powerless to stop them.

video:video.video:title: Kuwait releases surveillance video of deadly drone strike on its international airport
video:video.video:description: Footage from Kuwait's Directorate General of Civil Aviation showed the moment of impact from several angles.

video:video.video:title: About 500,000 people pour into a Madrid plaza for an evening prayer vigil with Pope Leo XIV
video:video.video:description: About 500,000 people, many of them young Spaniards, poured into a Madrid plaza for an evening prayer vigil with Pope Leo XIV on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Macron and Kagame attend inauguration of memorial to Rwanda's Tutsi genocide in Paris
video:video.video:description: French President Emmanuel Macron and Rwandan President Paul Kagame on Tuesday inaugurated a monument in Paris in memory of the victims of the Rwanda genocide.

video:video.video:title: Head of UN mission in DRCongo visits Bunia to address response to Ebola outbreak
video:video.video:description: James Swan, who heads the nearly 9,000-member U.N. peacekeeping force in Congo known as MONUSCO, said he wanted to make sure UN personnel were respecting health protocols.

video:video.video:title: US military’s Central Command says it launched more strikes on Iran on second day of renewed fire
video:video.video:description: US military’s Central Command says it launched more strikes on Iran on second day of renewed fire

video:video.video:title: Beijing's E-Town home to dozens of robotic research, development and manufacturing companies
video:video.video:description: The robots stand ready to demonstrate their capabilities – driving screws to assemble component, sorting out delivery packages, or dancing to entertain human visitors.

video:video.video:title: Cuba's iconic antique cars sit idle as US oil blockade deepens fuel crisis
video:video.video:description: A worsening fuel crisis across Cuba is testing the island's famed "almendrones," the vintage American cars that serve as vital shared taxis and embody the island’s ingenuity and endurance.

video:video.video:title: Nvidia bets on AI personal computers with new chip powering Windows laptops
video:video.video:description: Nvidia unveiled on Monday new powerful chips that would bring advanced artificial intelligence functions into laptops and desktop computers, with the new personal computer models from brands including Microsoft and Dell set to roll out later this year.

video:video.video:title: Bill Gates testifies to House Oversight Committee about his ties to Epstein, AP explains
video:video.video:description: Bill Gates says he made an error in judgment by ever meeting with Jeffrey Epstein as the Microsoft co-founder faces questions behind closed doors from lawmakers about his relationship with the disgraced financier.

video:video.video:title: Nvidia CEO throws ceremonial first pitch at S.Korean baseball game
video:video.video:description: Nvidia founder and CEO Jensen Huang threw the ceremonial first pitch at a South Korean baseball game in Seoul.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 3
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Wednesday, June 3rd: Iranian attack on Kuwait’s airport reported; Massive aerial attack in Ukraine; California holds primary in race for Governor; L.A. mayor goes to runoff in reelection bid.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 2
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Tuesday, June 2nd: Massive Russian attack across Ukraine; Trump says Israel and Hezbollah to dial back fighting; Senate Republicans want answers about Trump settlement fund; Shooting suspect and six others dead in Iowa town.

video:video.video:title: Protest in Tirana over a coastal development project linked to Jared Kushner
video:video.video:description: Protest in Tirana over a coastal development project linked to Jared Kushner

video:video.video:description: The Vatican says that Pope Leo XIV has met with six survivors of clergy sexual abuse in Madrid and vowed to consider their suggestions for how the Catholic Church can improve its response to the crisis.

video:video.video:title: Bodies removed in Johannesburg after a dozen people killed by attackers in late-night mass shooting
video:video.video:description: South African police say that multiple attackers killed 12 people and wounded at least nine in a late-night mass shooting in the city of Johannesburg.

video:video.video:description: Jennifer Lopez says even if past relationships have not worked out, she hasn't closed the door on meeting future love interests at work. (June 5)

video:video.video:description: The U.S. military has attacked another boat accused of smuggling drugs in the eastern Pacific Ocean, killing two men.

video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, June 10th: Iran launches attacks on Kuwait, Bahrain; Graham Platner wins Maine Democratic Senate nomination; Burning cross found in Chicago park; Bill Gates to testify to House Committee about Epstein files.

video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV is expected to visit Barcelona next week and give a blessing for the iconic Sagrada Familia's Tower of Jesus Christ that made the basilica the world’s tallest church when it was raised in October.

video:video.video:description: A fire at a nursing home in western Sri Lanka killed 12 residents and injured eight others, police said Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Trump says pilots are fine after US helicopter crashes near Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: U.S. President Donald Trump says the pilots of a helicopter that crashed around the Strait of Hormuz are “fine."

video:video.video:title: Survivors share experiences and lessons from Congo’s 2018 Ebola outbreak
video:video.video:description: The memories come flooding back whenever Vianney Kambale Kombi hears the word Ebola. He remembers the pain and fear in his community in the eastern Congo city of Beni during the 2018-2020 Ebola outbreak, history's second-biggest with more than 3,400 reported cases and over 2,200 deaths.

video:video.video:title: Prime minister and Serbian opposition leader vote in Kosovo elections
video:video.video:description: Kosovo's Prime Minister cast his ballot on Sunday as voters headed to the polls for an early parliamentary election, the third in less than 18 months, in an attempt to unlock a political impasse in the small Balkan nation.

video:video.video:title: Federal judge strikes down Trump’s $100,000 fee on new H-1B visas
video:video.video:description: A federal judge struck down the Trump administration's $100,000 H-1B visa fee, reversing a previous court decision that upheld the hike designed to protect American jobs.

video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump’s warnings at the White House and on social media came hours after Bahrain, Kuwait and Jordan — all of which host U.S. troops — came under Iranian fire.

video:video.video:description: In response to a question from The Associated Press during a meeting with heads of international news agencies, the Russian president also said his country is open for a compromise on Ukraine in line with understandings reached during his summit with President Trump in Alaska.

video:video.video:title: Northern English town set for by-election which may give Andy Burnham a path to be next UK leader
video:video.video:description: Nearly 75,000 voters in Ashton-in-Makerfield will cast ballots in a contest that may well pick the UK’s next prime minister, or plunge Britain's febrile politics into even more turmoil.

video:video.video:description: Damaging storms are sweeping through the Midwest, disrupting flights at Chicago airports and knocking out power to hundreds of thousands of customers.

video:video.video:title: Protesters, police clash in Bolivia after president signs law enabling a harsher crackdown
video:video.video:description: Protesters in Bolivia demanding the resignation of conservative President Rodrigo Paz hurled firecrackers, stones and sticks at police who responded with tear gas on Monday, leading to dozens of arrests as road blockades continue to paralyze the Andean nation.

video:video.video:description: Peruvian presidential candidates Roberto Sánchez and Keiko Fujimori voted in Lima on Sunday as the nation went to the polls to decide their ninth president in just 10 years.

video:video.video:title: Taylor Swift at Knicks vs Spurs
video:video.video:description: Stars including Taylor Swift and A$AP Rocky attend Knicks' record-breaking rally over Spurs in Game 4 of the NBA Final.

video:video.video:title: Purported missiles were seen over the West Bank after a renewed exchange of fire between Israel and Iran.
video:video.video:description: Smoke from interceptions was visible and explosions were heard in Jerusalem, and what appeared to be missiles were seen over the West Bank Monday morning after a renewed exchange of fire between Israel and Iran.

video:video.video:title: North Korean leader Kim showcases new warship ahead of visit by China's Xi
video:video.video:description: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un observed sea trials of a new warship repaired after a failed launch last year and vowed to accelerate efforts to build a nuclear-armed navy, state media said Saturday, showcasing his expanding military capabilities ahead of a visit by Chinese leader Xi Jinping.

video:video.video:title: WHO chief reports 5 Ebola recoveries as a new treatment center opens in eastern Congo
video:video.video:description: The World Health Organization has reported five patients have recovered from a rare type of Ebola virus in eastern Congo.

video:video.video:title: Homeland security secretary defends decision on World Cup referee from Somalia
video:video.video:description: Homeland Security Secretary Markwayne Mullin is defending his department's decision to deny a World Cup referee from Somalia entry to the U.S.

video:video.video:title: FIFA's Infantino says Iran, ticketing and visas are main issues on eve of World Cup
video:video.video:description: On the eve of the start of the World Cup FIFA's president acknowledge three main concerns outside of soccer's realm which had been dominating headlines ahead of the tournament.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories June 5
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, June 5th: UN force clears rubble in Lebanon town; Ukrainians react positively to Zelenskyy's letter to Putin; EU leaders meet to discuss the Balkans; so-called Leonardo's ferry helps river crossing in Italy.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV departs for Spain for a 7-day Apostolic visit
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV departed for Spain on Saturday for a 7-day Apostolic visit. Leo is expected to double down on his messages of unity amid polarization, peace as war rages, welcome for migrants and hope for young Spaniards in the era of artificial intelligence.

video:video.video:title: Steampunk festival brings Victorian style and sci-fi oddity to New Zealand town
video:video.video:description: Steam-powered vehicles and Victorian costumes have taken over the New Zealand town of Ōamaru for its annual Steampunk Festival.

video:video.video:title: New York says it is prepared for World Cup events
video:video.video:description: New York says it is prepared for the upcoming World Cup games and will deploy additional law enforcement to aid in the patrolling of major transit hubs and other special events.

video:video.video:title: ICC chief prosecutor suspended pending decision by oversight body on sexual misconduct allegations
video:video.video:description: In an unprecedented move, the embattled chief prosecutor of the International Criminal Court was suspended from his duties late Monday, after the court’s oversight body referred British barrister Karim Khan for disciplinary proceedings.

video:video.video:title: Hong Kong customs swoop ahead of the World Cup, seizing $20M in fake goods
video:video.video:description: Hong Kong authorities announced, just hours before the World Cup was set to kick off, the seizure of 230,000 suspected counterfeit items worth an estimated $20 million, including jerseys tied to the highly anticipated soccer tournament.

video:video.video:title: Street in The Hague drenched in orange as excitement builds in Netherlands for World Cup
video:video.video:description: As the clock ticks down to the Netherlands' opening World Cup match, the country is gripped once more by orange fever.

video:video.video:title: Air Canada pilot charged after allegedly flying without proper license between 2009 and 2025
video:video.video:description: Geoffrey Wall, of Barrie, Ontario, is alleged to have operated as an airline captain between 2009 and 2025 without a license to fly large commercial passenger planes, according to Peel Regional Police.

video:video.video:title: At 86, Al Pacino reflects
video:video.video:description: Al Pacino looks back as he promotes “In the Hand of Dante" - his second film at Tribeca.

video:video.video:title: Russian strike on Sumy region damages civilian infrastructure
video:video.video:description: A Russian drone struck a five-storey residential building in the north eastern Ukrainian city of Konotop in the Sumy region on Monday, State Emergency Services of Ukraine said.

video:video.video:title: Engineers build AI-powered ultrasound wristband that enables realtime control of robotic hands, VR
video:video.video:description: Scientists have developed an ultrasound wristband that uses artificial intelligence to translate images of the wrist into real-time control of a dexterous robotic hand – or manipulate objects in a virtual environment.

video:video.video:title: Sherpa guide missing on Mount Everest for days rescued and airlifted to hospital
video:video.video:description: A Sherpa guide was found crawling to base camp on Mount Everest a week after he went missing, and was reunited with his family who had given up hope he would return.

video:video.video:title: People in earthquake-hit Philippines city call for help to meet basic needs
video:video.video:description: The mayor of a quake-hit southern Philippine town is pleading for air force helicopters to transport food to stave off hunger in places isolated by landslides.

video:video.video:title: Police investigate Iowa man suspected of shooting 6 of his relatives and then himself
video:video.video:description: Authorities in Iowa are investigating the fatal shootings of six people who they believe were killed by a relative who took his own life when confronted by police Monday.

video:video.video:title: Dua Lipa and Callum Turner host wedding party in Palermo
video:video.video:description: After a London civil ceremony, pop star Dua Lipa and actor Callum Turner hosted a wedding party in Palermo with friends and family over the weekend. (June 8)

video:video.video:title: Pope highlights migrants' plight, urges humility, on Canary Islands
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV has traveled to the Canary Islands, an epicenter of the European migration debate. He challenged countries to uphold migrants’ rights while shaming those leaders, including Christians, who turn them away with indifference.

video:video.video:title: White House previews the cage-match arena for upcoming UFC spectacle
video:video.video:description: The White House hosts an official media walk through of the South Lawn where a UFC mixed martial arts cage-match will be held for Trump’s 80th birthday.

video:video.video:title: Prosecutors in au pair double murder case react to Virginia man's life sentence
video:video.video:description: Fairfax County prosecutor speaks after Brendan Banfield was sentenced to life in prison without parole for the murder of his wife and a man who was lured to the couple’s home as a fall guy.

video:video.video:title: Israel strikes Iran after taking missile fire, AP Explains
video:video.video:description: Israel struck Iran on Monday after being targeted by missiles, while a U.S. military base in Saudi Arabia came under fire in the most serious exchange of hostilities since an April ceasefire, raising the possibility of a return to heavy fighting and complicating mediation efforts to end the war.

video:video.video:title: Why Leo's first encyclical went viral and how it speaks to his papal approach
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV has issued a manifesto calling for robust regulation of artificial intelligence. His first encyclical, “Magnifica Humanitas,” has sparked widespread online reactions.

video:video.video:title: Taiwan holds beach drills simulating a Chinese amphibious invasion
video:video.video:description: Taiwan’s army conducted live-fire beach drills Tuesday simulating a Chinese amphibious invasion, as the island strengthens its defenses amid military pressure from China.

video:video.video:title: Rumors and mistrust persist as eastern DRC battles Ebola outbreak
video:video.video:description: Various rumors and mistrust have persisted in the eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo as the region battles an Ebola outbreak. (AP Video by Justin Kabumba)

video:video.video:title: Colombia's presidential runoff could reshape the future of the Amazon and fossil fuel development
video:video.video:description: The upcoming Colombian presidential election between Sen. Iván Cepeda, an ally of President Gustavo Petro, and lawyer Abelardo de la Espriella, whom U.S.

video:video.video:title: Ecuadorian artisans struggle to preserve traditional hand-woven strainers
video:video.video:description: Artisans in Guangopolo, Ecuador, are struggling to keep the traditional craft of weaving horsehair sieves alive. 50 years ago, about 500 families made a living from the craft, selling up to 600 sieves a month.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories June 9
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Tuesday, June 9: Trump says US must respond to downed helicopter; Pope Leo greets thousands in Barcelona; Protests in Kenya over US proposed Ebola center; Carney says Canadian-built bridge across Detroit River that Trump threatened will open.

video:video.video:title: 'We'll sell to someone else': Lula defiant after Trump administration proposes 25% tariffs on Brazil
video:video.video:description: Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva remained defiant on Tuesday after the Trump administration proposed 25% tariffs on imports from Brazil.

video:video.video:title: Nora Fatehi’s key ingredients for a World Cup song: rhythm, celebration, and a winning feel
video:video.video:description: World Cup opening ceremony performer, Nora Fatehi reveals what fans can expect from her FIFA 2026 official song ‘Siir Siir.' (June 11)

video:video.video:title: Videos showing groups of people entering NYC sewers at night baffle residents and investigators
video:video.video:description: A series of bizarre sightings of people popping in and out of New York’s vast, subterranean sewer system in recent days has the whole city wondering what exactly is going on.

video:video.video:title: Attorney Ben Crump calls for upgraded charges in North Carolina police assault case

video:video.video:title: Residents flee Beirut's southern suburbs after Israel orders strikes
video:video.video:description: Traffic jams were seen in Southern Beirut, Monday, as residents attempted to flee after Israel ordered strikes on Dahiyeh where Hezbollah enjoys widespread support.

video:video.video:description: Traffic jams were seen in Southern Beirut, Monday, as residents attempted to flee after Israel ordered strikes on Dahiyeh where Hezbollah enjoys widespread support.

video:video.video:title: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth arrives in Cuba in shorts and t-shirt
video:video.video:description: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth arrived at Leeward Point Field in Guantanamo Bay, Cuba Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Traveling during a heat wave? Tips for staying safe
video:video.video:description: Planning a summer getaway? Make sure to take precautions when traveling during extreme summer heat. Sizzling temperatures can cause heat exhaustion and life-threatening heat stroke.

video:video.video:title: Trump on fate of anti-weaponization fund: 'I don't know'
video:video.video:description: In his first public comments since his Justice Department said a controversial $1.8 billion anti-weaponization fund would not go forward, the president equivocated.

video:video.video:title: A century since birth of Hollywood icon Marilyn Monroe
video:video.video:description: It's been a century since the birth of Hollywood icon Marilyn Monroe on June 1, 1926.

video:video.video:title: Amnesty accuses Israel's government of 'ethnic cleansing' of Palestinians from the West Bank
video:video.video:description: Amnesty International accused Israel on Wednesday of carrying out a campaign of “ethnic cleansing” of Palestinians from the occupied West Bank with the intention to annex the Palestinian territory.

video:video.video:title: Michelle Pfeiffer says she learned 'OnlyFans' can be 'all kinds of things'
video:video.video:description: Michelle Pfeiffer, who stars in 'Margo's Got Money Troubles,' says the show helped her learn more about the online platform.

video:video.video:title: Peruvians are soon to choose their president, but neither candidate has convinced the electorate
video:video.video:description: Peru has two options when they head to the polls on Sunday: Keiko Fujimori of the Fuerza Popular political party and Roberto Sánchez of the Juntos por el Perú political party, who, out of more than 30 presidential candidates in the first round, were the top two choices.

video:video.video:title: Yemen’s Houthi rebels claim missile attack on Israel
video:video.video:description: Yemen’s Iran-backed Houthi rebels claimed an attack on Israel on Monday and said Israel-affiliated vessels would again be a target in the Red Sea, putting the waterway, as well as the Gulf of Aden and the narrow Bab el-Mandeb Strait connecting them, in danger.

video:video.video:title: Activists protest in Abuja, demand rescue of abducted schoolchildren
video:video.video:description: Nigerian activists and civil society campaigners on Thursday staged a protest in Abuja demanding the rescue of pupils and teachers abducted from schools in Oyo State, as concerns grow over recurring kidnappings targeting educational institutions across the country.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories June 10
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Wednesday, June 10th: Trump says Tehran will 'pay the price' for stalled talks; US Inflation hits 4.2% in May; Mass shooting kills 12 and injures 9 in a Johannesburg suburb; Scientists discover a deep whale graveyard.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian regions near the frontlines are seeing a rise in premature births
video:video.video:description: A rising number of babies are being born prematurely — before 37 weeks of pregnancy — in Ukraine, particularly in regions near the frontlines, where some areas have seen rates nearly double since the conflict started with Russia's invasion in 2022.

video:video.video:title: The Nigerian army frees 360 abducted people in northeastern Borno state
video:video.video:description: The Nigerian army said Sunday it freed 360 people abducted by Boko Haram in southern Borno, in the northeastern part of the country.

video:video.video:title: Knicks fans react after Game 3 NBA Finals loss vs Spurs
video:video.video:description: New York Knicks fans gathered in Manhattan’s Bryant Park stayed optimistic after the basketball team lost Game 3 of the NBA Finals to the San Antonio Spurs 115-111 on Monday night.

video:video.video:title: Honda recalls over 880,000 cars due to rear suspension issue
video:video.video:description: Honda is recalling more than 800,000 vehicles because rear suspension components may fail and cause drivers to lose control, increasing the chances of a crash or injury.

video:video.video:title: India's viral Cockroach Party launches nationwide youth protests
video:video.video:description: India’s viral Cockroach Janta Party launched a nationwide protest campaign Thursday. The group is calling for the education minister's resignation after alleged examination irregularities.

video:video.video:title: DHS secretary praises state cooperation at New Jersey ICE facility
video:video.video:description: Markwayne Mullin says if needed, he has a plan to pull CBP officers to help with security at the Delaney Hall facility, but says the state is working to provide security there so it’s not needed right now.

video:video.video:title: Tom Hanks, Tim Allen launch 'Toy Story 5'
video:video.video:description: Joan Cusack, Wallace Shawn and Tony Hale among cast gathering to launch the latest adventure in the much loved franchise, in Los Angeles. (June 10)

video:video.video:title: Lonnie Ali reflects on Muhammad Ali's legacy of compassion 10 years after his death
video:video.video:description: The Ali Center in Muhammad Ali's hometown of Louisville, Kentucky, is sponsoring a “Day of Compassion” this week to promote acts of service and caring.

video:video.video:title: Former White House chief of staff's bike tour in New Hampshire tests a 2028 presidential bid
video:video.video:description: For Rahm Emanuel, the road to the White House runs through the uphill climbs of rural New Hampshire.

video:video.video:title: Steven Spielberg is convinced there's alien life
video:video.video:description: As Steven Spielberg returns with new sci-fi movie "Disclosure Day," the director explains why his long-held belief in alien life has turned into a conviction. (June 3)

video:video.video:title: Kansas City fan festival welcomes fans for the opening match of the World Cup
video:video.video:description: Soccer fans flocked to Kansas City’s FIFA World Cup Festival watch the opening match of the tournament between Mexico and South Africa. (AP video by Nick Ingram)

video:video.video:title: UN chief warns escalating attacks and rhetoric risk 'full war' in Iran and Gulf region
video:video.video:description: António Guterres told the U.N. Security Council Wednesday that the ceasefire in the Gulf region “is more like a lesser fire,” as the world has witnessed in the last 48 hours, with the downing of a U.S. helicopter, and the U.S. and Iran now trading attacks.

video:video.video:title: Senate passes $70B immigration bill enforcement bill in new funding for ICE, Border Patrol
video:video.video:description: The Senate passed legislation to fund President Donald Trump’s immigration enforcement agencies early Friday, after weeks of delays and fierce backlash to an unrelated $1.776 billion settlement fund that threatened to derail the bill.

video:video.video:title: Jerome Powell strongly defends Fed independence while accepting JFK Profile in Courage Award
video:video.video:description: Former Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell used one of his first major public appearances since stepping down as chairman to strongly defend the independence of the central bank, saying that could destroy "priceless" credibility.

video:video.video:title: Deadly Israeli strikes reported in southern Lebanon as tensions remain high
video:video.video:description: Deadly Israeli strikes were reported in southern Lebanon on Tuesday, as tensions remain high despite U.S.

video:video.video:title: Are wired headphones cool again? We asked AP staffers
video:video.video:description: Wired headphones are having a moment again, with some people embracing them for their reliability, sound quality and freedom from battery anxiety.

video:video.video:title: Fire at medical equipment warehouse in California prompts evacuations at nearby warehouses
video:video.video:description: A fire at a medical equipment warehouse in Northern California sent thick black smoke pouring into the sky Thursday and prompted evacuations of other nearby facilities as authorities fought to get the blaze under control.

video:video.video:title: Da Vinci-era ferry helps commuters beat everyday traffic
video:video.video:description: A 500-year-old ferry dating back to Leonardo da Vinci’s era is once again carrying commuters across the River Adda, in northern Italy.

video:video.video:title: Paul Stanley credits teamwork for the lasting success of KISS
video:video.video:description: Stanley says "the love of the band" helps sustain his working relationship with Gene Simmons. (June 11)

video:video.video:title: Pop-up art show takes over German president's residence before years-long renovation
video:video.video:description: A pop-up art show featuring contemporary works is set to open at the German president's official residency this week before renovations begin.

video:video.video:title: Rapper Ye holds concert in the Dutch city of Arnhem despite opposition from Jewish groups
video:video.video:description: A concert by rapper Ye, formerly Kanye West, took place in the eastern Dutch city of Arnhem on Saturday despite vocal opposition from Jewish groups and many Dutch lawmakers.

video:video.video:title: P!NK's Tony Awards plan is simple: 'Don't screw up'
video:video.video:description: P!NK is raising her glass to a new challenge. The Grammy-winning performer is stepping into the spotlight as host of the 79th Annual Tony Awards.

video:video.video:title: Ian McKellen honors the home of Laurence Olivier

video:video.video:description: A packed Danish crowd Saturday celebrated the much-maligned but enduring mullet hairstyle at the country’s annual Mullet Championships. (AP Video by James Brooks)

video:video.video:description: Iran's national football team intensified their preparations on Wednesday ahead of FIFA World Cup, holding an energetic training session in Antalya, Turkey as excitement builds across their country ahead of the tournament.

video:video.video:title: Police respond to scene where multiple people were shot near a festival in Toledo, Ohio
video:video.video:description: Multiple people were shot Saturday afternoon near a community street festival in Toledo, Ohio, police said. Officers are actively searching for suspects.

video:video.video:title: Iranian football team arrives in Mexico for World Cup preparations
video:video.video:description: The Iranian football team arrived in Mexico amid high security early on Sunday for its World Cup preparations.

video:video.video:title: Kelli O'Hara enjoys looseness of 'Fallen Angels'
video:video.video:description: Kelli O'Hara and Rose Byrne co-starred in the revival of "Fallen Angels" on Broadway. (June 7)

video:video.video:title: Everyday life appears normal in Tehran, but Iran's central bank says inflation at WWII levels
video:video.video:description: Life appeared normal in Tehran on Tuesday, while the country's central banks says inflation has reached a level unseen since World War II, amid a shaky ceasefire with the United States and Israel.

video:video.video:title: Authorities say one person is dead and another injured after a train strikes a semi-truck in Iowa
video:video.video:description: A train has struck a semi-truck in eastern Iowa, killing one person and injuring another. Poweshiek County Sheriff Matt Maschmann said one person in the truck died, while another with serious injuries was taken to a hospital.

video:video.video:title: Keith Urban's 'bulletproof' new album
video:video.video:description: Keith Urban talks about his new album, "Flow State," which features 10 cover songs, guest appearances from John Mayer and Little Big Town, and one original track, "We Go Back," featuring Michael McDonald.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV visits prison on outskirts of Barcelona with message of hope for inmates
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV visited a Barcelona prison Wednesday, keeping up a tradition of Pope Francis, who often used his foreign trips to minister to those who can’t join in the public celebrations when a pope comes to town.

video:video.video:description: The U.S.-brokered agreement, announced in a joint statement by the U.S., Israel and Lebanon Wednesday, comes after Israeli forces made their deepest incursion into Lebanon in more than a quarter century.

video:video.video:title: Palestinians suffer from lack of proper toilets across Gaza's vast tent cities
video:video.video:description: Palestinians have been suffering from lack of proper toilets across Gaza's vast tent cities. In their bare-bones tent in southern Gaza, Mostafa Shaaban built his family’s makeshift toilet behind a curtain in a corner.

video:video.video:title: Supporters of Karmelo Anthony demonstrate as teen convicted of murder for stabbing another athlete

video:video.video:description: Beyond Venice’s crowded canals is a fragile lagoon ecosystem. The scientists and environmental groups working to restore these wetlands say a sharp rise in the number of flamingos may be a sign their efforts are paying off.

video:video.video:description: In a video shared by Mahayahay Elementary School on social media, school children were seen screaming and crying when an offshore magnitude 7.8 earthquake rocked the southern Philippines early Monday.

video:video.video:description: Josh O’Connor dishes on his favorite Steven Spielberg films, while Emily Blunt says the director's catalog is always a wonderful choice for a good movie night.

video:video.video:title: Congo's Ebola resurgence sparks fears of a repeat of 2018's deadly confusion in Beni
video:video.video:description: The second-biggest outbreak of Ebola in history took place in Congo’s North Kivu, South Kivu and Ituri provinces, with some cases in neighboring Uganda between 2018-2020.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 1
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Monday, June 1st: Kuwait says it activated air defenses due to missile and drone attack; Trump expresses optimism over U.S.

video:video.video:title: Pope urges solidarity with migrants and ethical oversight of military use of AI
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV called Monday for newfound respect for the rights of migrants and international law during an address to the Spanish parliament that signaled a new level of acceptance of the Catholic Church in the public sphere in the overwhelmingly secular country.

video:video.video:title: Albanian police and protesters scuffle on fourth day of protests over Trump family-linked resort
video:video.video:description: The fourth consecutive protest in the Albanian capital, Tirana, against a project linked to the Trump family turned violent on Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he 'wouldn't mind' slashing intelligence jobs
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump says he wants his new acting director of national intelligence to slash positions in the office, which has already been significantly scaled back during his second term.

video:video.video:title: “Schmigadoon!" wins best musical at Tony Awards
video:video.video:description: “Schmigadoon!,” an adaptation of an Apple TV series that gently mocks big, brassy Broadway shows, won the best new musical and actor John Lithgow, 80, became the oldest man ever to win a competitive acting Tony.

video:video.video:title: Residents at Lebanon-Israel border area voice mixed feelings amid ongoing efforts to end conflict
video:video.video:description: A day after U.S. President Donald Trump said Israel and Hezbollah agreed to dial back fighting, people in Beirut and northern Israel shared mixed reactions to the latest development.

video:video.video:title: Somali World Cup referee denied entry to US arrives home to hero's welcome
video:video.video:description: A World Cup referee from Somalia who was denied entry to the United States has returned to his country's capital of Mogadishu, where he was received by a crowd of supporters and officials.

video:video.video:title: Former first lady of France, Bernadette Chirac, dies aged 93
video:video.video:description: President Emmanuel Macron confirmed her death Saturday, saying he and his wife Brigitte had learned with “great sadness” of the passing of a woman who marked French history beside Jacques Chirac, who died in 2019, and changed the lives of millions of patients through her charitable work.
video:video.video:tag: Bernadette Thérèse Marie Chodron de Courcel

video:video.video:title: Six people injured in Russian strike on Odesa
video:video.video:description: Six people were injured in an overnight Russian drone attack in Odesa, Maryna Averina, from the State Emergency Service of Odesa region reported on Monday.

video:video.video:title: The highest bridge in the world invigorates remote mountain communities in China
video:video.video:description: Building the Huajiang Grand Canyon Bridge in Guizhou province, has been a massive engineering project.

video:video.video:title: US bombs Iranian military sites and Kuwait is hit by drone and missile fire, AP explains
video:video.video:description: Air sirens went off across Kuwait City earlier on Monday as Tehran struck Kuwait in response to the United States bombing military sites in Iran.

video:video.video:title: El Nino has formed in the Pacific and could set records, forecasters say
video:video.video:description: The weather pattern's effects vary by region. El Nino often dampens — but doesn't eliminate — Atlantic hurricane season activity, but increases it in the Pacific.

video:video.video:title: Pakistan army helicopter crashes in Kashmir due to technical fault, killing all on board
video:video.video:description: A Pakistani army MI-17 helicopter crashed due to a technical fault in Pakistan-controlled Kashmir on Wednesday, killing all military personnel on board, the military said.

video:video.video:title: Could your next job interview be with an AI bot?
video:video.video:description: Growing numbers of job applicants are no longer facing a human person in their first interview. Instead, candidates are confronted with AI chatbots designed to screen applications and assess answers before involving a human recruiter.

video:video.video:title: Protesters in Kenya call for national crisis to be declared over gender-based violence
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of protesters took to the streets of Kenya’s capital on Monday, calling for the government to urgently investigate rising cases of gender-based violence and femicide, or the killing of women.

video:video.video:title: World Cup begins in Mexico City
video:video.video:description: The largest World Cup in tournament history kicks off with co-host Mexico taking on South Africa in the opening match. Here's how fans feel.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 2
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Tuesday, June 2nd: President Trump signs executive order on artificial intelligence; Marco Rubio grilled on Capitol Hill; Iranian news reported Iran stopped communicating with mediators; Drug cartel tunnel discovered in California.

video:video.video:title: World Cup dream brings hope to Haiti amid violence and instability
video:video.video:description: Haiti’s qualification for the 2026 World Cup is offering many people across the country a rare sense of hope as the Caribbean nation struggles through one of the most difficult periods in its recent history.

video:video.video:title: Soccer offers West Bank Palestinians an escape as the World Cup kicks off
video:video.video:description: As the FIFA World Cup gets underway, with Egypt, Iraq, Jordan, Qatar and Saudi Arabia among the Arab nations set to compete, soccer remains both a passion and a struggle for many Palestinians in the occupied West Bank.

video:video.video:title: Ukrainian maritime drone explodes at a Romanian Black Sea port
video:video.video:description: A Ukrainian maritime drone that was being used in the country’s war against Russia exploded Friday at a Black Sea port in Romania, while three other sea drones exploded outside the port, Romanian authorities said.

video:video.video:title: Key takeaways from the high-stake races for California governor and LA mayor
video:video.video:description: The high-stakes races for California governor and LA mayor remained too close to call, but the battle lines for the November election are already being drawn as the candidates lay out their visions for leading the nation's most populous state.

video:video.video:title: Rare underwater footage of a Great White Shark in the Mediterranean
video:video.video:description: Divers captured underwater footage of a Great White Shark in the Mediterranean for the first time recently off the coast of Sicily, Italy.

video:video.video:title: Japanese city captures bear that caused fear and school closures
video:video.video:description: A wandering bear that scared residents of a Japanese city just north of Tokyo was captured on Tuesday after several days of panic during which all schools were closed.

video:video.video:title: Matt Groening celebrates the longevity of "The Simpsons"
video:video.video:description: Matt Groening and James L. Brooks celebrate the longevity of "The Simpsons," as producer Brooks is celebrated at the Peabody Awards.

video:video.video:title: Trump says 'I love the inflation,' claims US taking millions of barrels of Iranian oil
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump called the latest inflation numbers “great," insisting prices will “come down like a rock” once the war with Iran ends.

video:video.video:title: A fish fight off Florida tests Trump’s drive to deregulate America’s seas
video:video.video:description: A federal judge in Washington has blocked a plan to loosen rules for fishing red snapper in the Atlantic, halting what was expected to be the longest recreational snapper season in years.

video:video.video:title: Israel strikes Beirut's southern suburbs days after US-supported ceasefire deal
video:video.video:description: Israel struck Beirut's southern suburbs without warning on Sunday, days after a ceasefire agreement in Washington went into effect and despite a US request not to attack Lebanon's capital.

video:video.video:title: Thousands flee Lebanese city under renewed escalation and threat of Israeli strikes
video:video.video:description: Cars packed with mattresses, luggage and household belongings stretched for miles along Lebanon's coastal highway as residents fled the southern city of Tyre following a latest Israeli warning.

video:video.video:title: Bill Gates to testify before panel investigating the Jeffrey Epstein files
video:video.video:description: Bill Gates has arrived on Capitol Hill to appear before a congressional panel investigating the Jeffrey Epstein files, becoming the latest powerful figure linked to the disgraced financier to testify.

video:video.video:title: California GOP candidate Steve Hilton says ‘the argument for change is irresistible’
video:video.video:description: Republican candidate for California governor said on Tuesday he wanted to break the Democrats’ winning streak in the state. (AP video shot by: Eugene Garcia)

video:video.video:title: See one way experts are monitoring infectious disease risks during the World Cup
video:video.video:description: Millions of fans are expected to travel for the World Cup, with matches spanning North America. At Georgetown University, public health experts are setting up a hub to track if there are warning signs of an outbreak.

video:video.video:title: More swings for AI stocks drag Wall Street back on the roller coaster
video:video.video:description: Drops for AI stocks drowned out the benefit of lower oil prices, and most stocks in the S&P 500 rose. Treasury yields edged lower in the bond market.

video:video.video:title: Chile students clash with police while protesting Kast government
video:video.video:description: Thousands of students, teachers and social organizations marched through downtown Santiago on Wednesday to protest education budget cuts reforms by president José Antonio Kast, they say would undermine access to education and student representation.

video:video.video:title: Toronto neighborhood challenges World Cup visitors to join The Great Beaver Quest
video:video.video:description: Thousands are flocking to a Toronto park to catch a glimpse of a beaver sculpture named Doug that is part of a new World Cup-themed contest. (AP video: Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: A new generation of Elvis tribute artists compete in the king's hometown
video:video.video:description: Donning dazzling jumpsuits and black hair spray, a group of young Elvis tribute artists competed against one another last week as part of the Tupelo Elvis Festival.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV arrives in Madrid on tour of Spain
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV has arrived in Spain for his tour of Madrid, Barcelona and the Canary Islands.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he’ll stay as Kennedy Center chairman, hints at continued fight for restoration
video:video.video:description: After a federal judge blocked US President Donald Trump’s planned renovation of the Kennedy Center, Trump on Friday said he's staying involved in efforts to overhaul the building.

video:video.video:title: Ex-head of nutrition agency and officials arrested in Indonesia corruption probe
video:video.video:description: The recently dismissed head of Indonesia's National Nutrition Agency, Dadan Hindayana, was arrested on Wednesday on corruption charges related to a multi-billion-dollar free-meals program.

video:video.video:title: House vote fails to extend FISA spy tool as Friday deadline looms
video:video.video:description: The impasse could soon result in limitations on what intelligence the U.S. government can collect abroad just as World Cup games begin in cities around the country and ahead of celebrations for the nation’s 250th anniversary.

video:video.video:title: Indian minister says engagement with Myanmar will continue, as country's leader visits New Delhi
video:video.video:description: India said it will continue engaging with Myanmar after Prime Minister Narendra Modi held talks Monday with the leader of the country’s military-backed government, despite Western sanctions imposed after the military seized power in 2021 (AP video shot by Rishi Lekhi).

video:video.video:title: Building in General Santos City collapses amid a magnitude 7.8 earthquake in the Philippines
video:video.video:description: A magnitude 7.8 earthquake shook part of the southern Philippines early Monday, causing damage in a key coastal city, knocking down power and sparking a tsunami warning on some regional coasts.

video:video.video:title: In Iowa, water pollution is a health threat that also disrupts summer fun
video:video.video:description: Iowa’s waterways face significant pollution challenges due to agricultural runoff and sewage. Authorities say many lakes and streams do not meet standards for swimming, drinking or aquatic life.

video:video.video:title: A new generation of peregrine falcons fledge from their Sagrada Familia home
video:video.video:description: Two peregrine falcon fledglings have flown from their nests at the top of Barcelona’s famed Sagrada Familia, leaving behind crowds with binoculars, and live stream cameras which have been following their every move.

video:video.video:title: Serena Williams is returning to pro tennis at age 44 after nearly four years away
video:video.video:description: Serena Williams is returning to professional tennis at age 44, accepting a wild card entry into a doubles tournament nearly four years after her last competitive match and fueling speculation about a possible Wimbledon comeback.

video:video.video:title: Sub-Saharan Africa is the deadliest place in the world to have a baby, while its population booms
video:video.video:description: Maternal mortality in sub-Saharan Africa, which has the world’s fastest-growing population, accounts for 70% of global maternal deaths.

video:video.video:title: Highly effective prevention drug arrives in South Africa, which has world's highest HIV burden
video:video.video:description: Growing up witnessing the devastating effects of HIV in her family and community in South Africa pushed Olwam Plaatjie to start using preventive HIV medications three years ago.

video:video.video:title: Video shows UK police handcuffing teen after being stabbed, stirring race and knife crime debate
video:video.video:description: The fatal stabbing of a British teenager who was handcuffed by police while his killer stood nearby erupted into a debate Tuesday about policing, race and knife crime.

video:video.video:title: Extended bar hours seek to boost businesses during World Cup
video:video.video:description: Pennsylvania is one of many states signing off on extending bar and restaurant hours during the world’s most-watched sporting event. (AP Video: Tassanee Vejpongsa)

video:video.video:title: UN offers condolences for peacekeeper killed in Lebanon, welcomes ceasefire agreement
video:video.video:description: A Serbian UNIFIL peacekeeper was killed and two others were wounded when a mortar shell struck their location.

video:video.video:title: Thousands continue to protest Trump family-linked resort in Albania
video:video.video:description: Thousands of citizens gathered in Tirana for a 12th consecutive day of protests against a proposed luxury resort linked to Jared Kushner, the son-in-law of U.S.

video:video.video:title: Maine voters share their thoughts on US Senate candidates Graham Platner and Susan Collins
video:video.video:description: Maine voters have shared their thoughts on U.S. Senate candidates of their choice during the primary election on Tuesday. (AP Video shot by Rodrique Ngowi)

video:video.video:title: AP Explains: Impact of Brendan Sorsby's court win on college sports and gambling
video:video.video:description: Texas Tech transfer quarterback Brendan Sorsby has won a temporary injunction against the NCAA over his gambling suspension.

video:video.video:title: French-Iranian cartoonist and filmmaker Satrapi of 'Persepolis' fame dies aged 56
video:video.video:description: Acclaimed Iranian-French cartoonist and filmmaker Marjane Satrapi, a prominent advocate for women's rights and author of “Persepolis,” has died at 56, the French presidency said Thursday.

video:video.video:title: Destruction seen inside building in Philippines after 7.8 magnitude earthquake
video:video.video:description: An offshore magnitude 7.8 earthquake rocked the southern Philippines Monday, killing at least 19 people, and injuring over 200 others mostly in damaged buildings and sending a 1-meter (3-foot) tsunami into nearby coasts.

video:video.video:title: Death Cab for Cutie's Ben Gibbard: 'Fans are always right'
video:video.video:description: Death Cab for Cutie frontman Ben Gibbard explains why he paid so much attention to the band's fans when writing the indie group's 11th studio album, "I Built You a Tower."

video:video.video:title: Dozens brave bad weather in Taiwan to honour victims of Tiananmen crackdown
video:video.video:description: This year marks the 37th anniversary since the Chinese army ran tanks over protesters who gathered at the Tiananmen Square in Beijing to demand for more freedom and democracy.

video:video.video:title: World Cup excitement in Mexico tempered by security concerns
video:video.video:description: As Mexico prepares to host World Cup matches, excitement among fans and residents is being accompanied by questions about security and whether the country is ready for the influx of visitors.

video:video.video:title: Trump says he’s going to NBA Finals to root for the Knicks
video:video.video:description: Calling himself a “big fan” of the New York Knicks and owner James Dolan, President Donald Trump — born in Queens — said he’ll be attending at least one game at Madison Square Garden.

video:video.video:title: Albanians hold 10th of protest in Tirana against tourism project linked to Trump's son-in-law
video:video.video:description: Thousands of Albanians on Tuesday marched through the capital Tirana against a tourism development project linked to US President Donald Trump’s son-in-law, Jared Kushner.

video:video.video:title: A look at the hidden treasures of Barcelona's Sagrada Familia, ahead of Pope Leo's visit
video:video.video:description: The unique grandeur of the Sagrada Familia — the stone somehow carved to look lush as flora, the sandcastle-like spires, the color-flooded interior — means that even those who work and worship at Barcelona’s world famous basilica each and every day can still be surprised with some fresh wonder.

video:video.video:title: Xi makes rare visit to North korea as he and Kim seek stronger China-North Korea ties
video:video.video:description: China's President Xi Jinping and North Korea's leader Kim Jong Un underscored their commitment to deepen cooperation and rebuild their complicated traditional alliance.

video:video.video:title: US military disable oil tanker violating blockade in the Gulf of Oman, CENTCOM says
video:video.video:description: The U.S. military said Wednesday that it disabled an oil tanker in the Gulf of Oman. The Palau-flagged vessel was suspected of trying to violate the American blockade, the latest action by the Trump administration to try to push Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

video:video.video:title: Ike Barinholtz teases Madonna cameo in season two of ‘The Studio’
video:video.video:description: Speaking at the Peabody Awards, Ike Barinholtz discussed Madonna’s cameo in the upcoming second season of “The Studio," adding, "They wanted to go bigger and better in season two."

video:video.video:title: Ecuadorian artisans struggle to preserve traditional hand-woven strainers
video:video.video:description: Artisans in Guangopolo, Ecuador, are struggling to keep the traditional craft of weaving horsehair sieves alive.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories June 11
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Thursday, June 11th: Trump backs off new strikes against Iran; Labor Department reports big jump in producer prices; Trump announces new pick for director of national intelligence; World Cup fever strikes Mexico as tournament begins.

video:video.video:title: Larger-than-life looks at fashion graduate show
video:video.video:description: When it comes to dreaming big - and demonstrating it - few things are on the scale of the graduate fashion show for London arts college, Central Saint Martins. (June 5)

video:video.video:title: Sweden to ban mobiles in schools as part of back-to-books strategy
video:video.video:description: Long championed as a leader in tech and digital adoption, Sweden is set to ban mobile phones in schools from the next academic year as part of a broad reversal on the use of screens in classrooms.
video:video.video:tag: Organization for Economic Co-operation and Development

video:video.video:title: Rising gas prices could mean pricier summer for US boaters
video:video.video:description: U.S. boaters, like drivers, are feeling the pinch from the Iran war with higher gas costs. (AP video: Mike Householder and Dee-Ann Durbin)

video:video.video:title: Signs of revival of interest in faith among young people in Madrid, as pope visits
video:video.video:description: Traveling to Spain in June and France in September, Pope Leo XIV will find traditionally Catholic but now staunchly secular countries with an abundance of historic churches but few people in the pews.

video:video.video:title: Scenes of anguish as relatives bury Lebanese family killed in Israeli strike
video:video.video:description: Six members of the al-Abdallah family - father Hassan, mother Hanan, and four of their children, aged between 20 and six – died in Monday's air strike that demolished their home in Khiam, along the border with Israel.

video:video.video:title: Only on AP: Israeli ambassador to France says 'choice was Iran's to resume hostilities'
video:video.video:description: Israel's ambassador to France on Monday said the "choice was Iran's to resume hostilities" between Israel and Iran as both sides traded fire for the first time since the U.S. agreed to a ceasefire with Tehran two months ago.

video:video.video:title: 'Dancing Devils' chase children to their first communion in Venezuela
video:video.video:description: Children and spectators on Thursday were chased by 'dancing devils,' parishioners of the Carayaca church in Tarmas in Venezuela, as they took part in Corpus Christi celebrations (AP Video/Andry Rincon)

video:video.video:title: Lebanon and Israel extend fragile ceasefire, create security zones that exclude Hezbollah
video:video.video:description: In a joint statement released after a fourth round of U.S.-mediated talks at the State Department, the two sides said the ceasefire “is contingent on a complete cessation of Hezbollah fire and the evacuation of all Hezbollah operatives” from areas south of the Litani River, which is roughly 30 kilom

video:video.video:title: Animals at the Brookfield Zoo in Chicago gear up ahead of the World Cup
video:video.video:description: From its red river hogs to its Addax, Ike, to the American bison and its Grevy’s zebra, the animals at the zoo enjoyed soccer-themed enrichment activities last week ahead of the games.

video:video.video:title: Mexico fans gather at foot of Angel of Independence after World Cup win
video:video.video:description: Mexico City's iconic Ángel de la Independencia became the center of celebrations Thursday night after Mexico defeated South Africa 2-0 in the opening match of the 2026 World Cup.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo is greeted by enthusiastic crowds at soccer stadium in Madrid
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV visited the home of Real Madrid Football Club, the Santiago Bernabéu Stadium, where around 80,000 people burst into wild cheers as he entered the arena in an electric golf cart.

video:video.video:title: Demonstrators protest FIFA policy prohibiting pre-Islamic Revolution Iranian flags into World Cup matches
video:video.video:description: Waving the flags of pre-Islamic Revolution Iran, hundreds of protesters opposed to the current government demonstrated outside SOFI Stadium in Inglewood, California where the team's World Cup matches will be initially played.

video:video.video:title: Polls open in Ethiopia's election which is widely expected to be won by the ruling party
video:video.video:description: Polls opened Monday in Ethiopia in an election that is widely expected to be won by the ruling party.

video:video.video:title: Trump says US 'must' respond after Iran shot down US Army helicopter near Strait of Hormuz
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump blamed Iran for shooting down a U.S. Army helicopter near the Strait of Hormuz on Tuesday and said the United States must respond to the attack.

video:video.video:title: Israelis and Iranians weigh what comes next as countries appear to pause strikes
video:video.video:description: Israelis and Iranians showed conflicted emotions on Tuesday as the two countries appeared to end the latest exchange of strikes; some expressed optimism, while others remained skeptical about what comes next.

video:video.video:title: Pope Leo XIV visits a welfare center in Madrid run by the Caritas charity
video:video.video:description: Pope Leo XIV visited a welfare center run by the Caritas charity in a deprived area of Madrid on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Damage forces postponement of opening of 'cave' installation on Paris' oldest bridge
video:video.video:description: Parts of the installation, which looks like an inflatable cave, appeared ripped and were seen fluttering in high winds, with poor weather the likely culprit behind the damage, the project's media spokesperson said.

video:video.video:title: Only on AP: Under Notre Dame cathedral, a 'dig of the century' unearths 1,700 years of history
video:video.video:description: Archaeologists are digging beneath Notre Dame Cathedral to explore as far back as Roman Paris from 2,000 years ago.

video:video.video:title: Hong Kong artist braves police attention with commemoration on Tiananmen crackdown anniversary eve
video:video.video:description: A performance artist on Wednesday sought to display a thin red thread in downtown Hong Kong to remember the victims of Beijing’s 1989 Tiananmen Square crackdown, 37 years on from the event.

video:video.video:title: Three Siberian tiger cubs born at Ljubljana Zoo
video:video.video:description: Zoo staff members are monitoring the cubs’ development through cameras installed inside the enclosure and say that 4-year-old Arisa has adapted well to motherhood.

video:video.video:title: Bill Gates arrives to testify before panel investigating the Jeffrey Epstein files
video:video.video:description: Bill Gates has arrived on Capitol Hill to appear before a congressional panel investigating the Jeffrey Epstein files, becoming the latest powerful figure linked to the disgraced financier to testify.

video:video.video:title: Trump is scrapping $1.8B fund meant to compensate president's allies, Blanche says
video:video.video:description: The Trump administration is scrapping plans to create a $1.8 billion fund meant to compensate allies of the president, the Justice Department's top official said Tuesday.

video:video.video:title: Starmer hosts Zelenskyy, Macron and Merz for talks in London
video:video.video:description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy insisted Sunday that Europe must play a key role in any negotiations to end the war with Russia as he arrived for talks in London with staunch European allies in the wake of a Russian drone strike that killed three people.

video:video.video:title: Ukraine drones hit St. Petersburg oil terminal ahead of Putin's visit to city
video:video.video:description: Ukraine says its long-range drones struck an oil terminal in St. Petersburg, Russia and set it ablaze as the city hosted an annual international economic forum promoted by President Vladimir Putin.

video:video.video:title: Climate change is fueling more extreme weather
video:video.video:description: From stronger hurricanes to longer droughts and heavier rainfall, a warming planet is intensifying weather extremes and making them more frequent. (AP Video: Aya Diab)

video:video.video:title: Donkey therapy helps improve patient wellbeing at French hospital
video:video.video:description: Donkeys are helping psychiatric patients at a hospital in France. A unique therapy programme uses the animals to help people with mental health conditions recover.

video:video.video:title: Boots Riley on the bigger message behind 'I Love Boosters'
video:video.video:description: Boots Riley wants "I Love Boosters" to spark discussions around "how the working class can have power."

video:video.video:title: UK stabbing conviction prompts unrest in coastal town, and sparks fierce political debate
video:video.video:description: Police were pelted with chairs, cans, rocks and flares late Tuesday by some of the hundreds who attended a protest in the southern English coast city of Southampton, where Henry Nowak was killed in December.

video:video.video:title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang calls on Korean gaming superstar Faker during Seoul visit
video:video.video:description: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang began his five-day to South Korea on Friday by calling on international gaming superstar Faker.

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories June 8
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Monday, June 8th: Iran and Israel trade weapons fire; Houthi rebels claim missile fired at Israel from Yemen; Powerful earthquake in Philippines; Toledo, Ohio police search for mass shooting suspects.

video:video.video:title: Strong winds damage buildings and tear trees in Wisconsin as US Midwest braces for severe storms
video:video.video:description: Aerial footage shows cleanup crew cleaning up debris, roofs torn off buildings and trees split in half after a strong storm swept through a Wisconsin city.

video:video.video:title: Lebanese Americans do what they can to assist friends, family suffering due to attacks on homeland
video:video.video:description: Lebanese Americans pray, protest and raise money to help friends and family impacted by the fighting between Israel and Hezbollah. (AP video: Mike Householder)

video:video.video:title: Protests continue in Albania over tourism project linked to Trump’s son-in-law
video:video.video:description: Protests continued in Albania, Monday, against a tourism project linked to U.S. President Donald Trump’s son-in-law, Jared Kushner. (AP Video shot by Eri Xhabafti)

video:video.video:title: Police: Suspect who took 10 people hostage in California standoff has been shot and killed
video:video.video:description: A man held 10 people hostage inside a California office building before the FBI shot and killed him early Wednesday.

video:video.video:title: Dallas Stadium unveils World Cup transformation ahead of opening matches
video:video.video:description: Dallas Stadium will host the first North Texas World Cup match on June 14th between the Netherlands and Japan.

video:video.video:title: Oz says he trusts Trump’s judgement when asked why Pulte is qualified for DNI
video:video.video:description: Mehmet Oz, head of the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services, had his turn at the White House briefing room podium but had little to offer when reporters asked about Bill Pulte's nomination as acting director of national intelligence.

video:video.video:title: Author Ann Patchett drives the literary conversation
video:video.video:description: From her bookstore in Nashville, Tennessee, author Ann Patchett is showcasing other literary greats, while continuing her work as a prize-winning novelist with several bestsellers.

video:video.video:title: Armenian PM and opposition vote in election, amid Russian pressure to prevent drift toward West
video:video.video:description: Armenians are voting Sunday in parliamentary elections as the incumbent government, under mounting Russian pressure, seeks to loosen ties with Moscow and deepen cooperation with the West.

video:video.video:title: The Chinese empress dress trend soaring in Beijing
video:video.video:description: Studios near the Forbidden City in Beijing are transforming visitors into empresses from ancient China as young people explore a revival of traditional culture.

video:video.video:title: Emergency flood warning issued as Tropical Storm Jangmi makes landfall in western Japan
video:video.video:description: A tropical storm is dumping heavy rain and raising flood risks around east-central Japan as it moved into the heavily populated Tokyo region.

video:video.video:title: Democrat Xavier Becerra says he overcame underdog status in race for governor
video:video.video:description: Two Democrats and a Republican were leading in early returns Tuesday in California's crowded primary for governor, a campaign that tested voters' appetites for an experienced politician or candidates promising change.

video:video.video:title: Spanish hotel chain Meliá to shutter hotels in Cuba in fresh blow to island's limping tourism sector
video:video.video:description: Spanish hotel chain Meliá has joined a growing list of companies with a long-standing presence in Cuba that are withdrawing or limiting their presence on the island.

video:video.video:title: USDA official confirms a suspected case of the New World screwworm fly in south Texas
video:video.video:description: The U.S. Department of Agriculture has confirmed that the New World screwworm fly has arrived in south Texas.

video:video.video:title: Julia Louis-Dreyfus on June Squibb's miraculous career
video:video.video:description: Julia Louis-Dreyfus says she's inspired by fellow actor June Squibb. (June 7)

video:video.video:title: Lawsuit filed to stop UFC fight at the White House for Trump's birthday
video:video.video:description: A federal lawsuit is attempting to halt the UFC fight card on the White House South Lawn timed for President Donald Trump’s 80th birthday and part of the celebration of the nation’s 250th anniversary.

video:video.video:title: Denmark's annual championship celebrates the mullet hairstyle
video:video.video:description: Business in the front, party in the back - a packed Danish crowd Saturday celebrated the much-maligned but enduring mullet hairstyle at the country’s annual Mullet Championships.

video:video.video:title: 8 crested ibises released in Japan decades after extinction
